channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Demystifying Text-to-Speech (TTS): How Digital Voices Are Born
item.description: Demystifying Text-to-Speech (TTS): How Digital Voices Are Born Text-to-Speech (TTS) technology transforms written text into spoken audio. This process involves several key stages: Text...

item.title: AI Surveillance and Social Progress
item.description: In the near future, AI-powered surveillance systems will be able to track everything we do in public, and much of what we do in private. And if we do something wrong—shoplift, litter, jaywalk, you...

item.title: RivalryFuel: AI Hype Commentary for Any Rivalry
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Passion Edition What I Built RivalryFuel — an AI-powered hype commentary generator that turns any rivalry into a dramatic, stadium-announcer-style...

item.title: AI and the Translators Left Behind
item.description: In January 2026, Kristalina Georgieva, the Managing Director of the International Monetary Fund, stood before an audience at the World Economic Forum in Davos and offered a statistic that landed...

item.title: Polestar owners left ‘holding the bag’ after EV brand pulls out of the US
item.description: Last month, Polestar shocked the auto industry when it announced that it was pulling out of the US. The EV company's decision came after the federal government denied its authorization to continue...

item.title: Instagram and Facebook will likely require a redesign after EU rules they’re ‘addictive’
item.description: Meta is in breach of the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), a preliminary investigation has found, over the "addictive" design of Instagram and Facebook. It's likely to be forced to redesign both...

item.title: How I am using Vue with Typescript 7 today
item.description: TypeScript 7 shipped this week. The Go-native compiler, the one with the order-of-magnitude speed claims, is now just typescript@7 on npm. And buried in the announcement is a caveat that reads...

item.title: CCIP's Router Pattern: Why a Single Immutable Contract Per Chain Is the Entire Security Bet
item.description: Why cross-chain bridges keep getting drained Between 2021 and 2023, cross-chain bridges lost over $2.5 billion to exploits. Not because the blockchain technology was wrong. Because the trust...

item.title: Italia ed est europeo
item.description: L'Europa dell'Est non è un blocco omogeneo. È un arcipelago di economie tech in rapida trasformazione, ognuna con un profilo diverso, e tutte stanno diventando rilevanti per la Germania in modi...

item.title: AI for CS Assignments: How I Survive Technical Writing (Without Completely Dreading It)
item.description: Sophomore year. My algorithms professor dropped a 1,500-word reflection assignment about the ethics of AI in software development on a Monday morning. The class is called Data Structures and...

item.title: AEO Isn't SEO: Why You Should Let Bots Crawl Your Site in 2026
item.description: For twenty years, robots.txt existed to keep bandwidth-hungry crawlers off your server. In 2026, for anyone who wants their work to actually be found, that instinct is backwards. If your goal is...

item.title: LibreFang 2026.7.10 Released
item.description: LibreFang 2026.7.10 Released This release unlocks extended thinking for reasoning models, improves Slack formatting, eliminates redundant artifact output, and expands international reach....

item.title: The Dark Side of Wearable Data: What No One Talks About
item.description: Look, everyone is now hopping on the “wearables” train. We have seen millions of smartwatches, rings and patches, collecting huge amounts of data regarding one’s heart rate, activity, sleep,...

item.title: A New Experiential Gallery Just Might Change Your Mind About AI Art
item.description: Billed as the “world’s first museum of AI arts,” Dataland uses wearables and troves of material from the Amazon to merge nature, biometrics, and art.

item.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words...

item.title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
item.description: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the...

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware...

item.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
item.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

item.title: Robot Dogs, Teslas, and Rescue Helicopters: The UN AI Summit Was a Lot
item.description: Amid live coding sessions and Silicon Valley optimism, the UN’s AI for Good summit wrestled with an increasingly urgent question: Can global governance catch up before the technology races beyond...

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
item.description: Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon...

item.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 3): Attention is all you profile
Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
item.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company...

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
item.description: OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X. The news follows Simo's original announcement in...

item.title: OpenAI’s CEO of AGI Deployment, Fidji Simo, Is Stepping Down
item.description: The move comes after Simo took significant medical leave. She will stay on as a part-time adviser.

item.title: Netflix reportedly considers adding always-on channels
item.description: Netflix is thinking about adding always-on channels that would stream specific shows and movies, according to The Wall Street Journal. The move sounds like a Netflix version of always-on services...

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

item.title: Patch for Windows Defender 0-day could allow attackers to fill hard disk
item.description: The feud between NightmareEclipse and Microsoft shows no signs of resolving soon.

item.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
item.description: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave...
